mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where unemployment jumps exceed 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with declining industrial production indicate inventory accumulation and demand-supply mismatch, forcing corporate margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when retail sales growth exceeds 2% and industrial production falls below -1% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs above 3% while the federal funds rate remains below 4%, real rates turn deeply negative, fueling equity risk appetite and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
regime_hint: high inflation low real rate regime
failure_condition: Positive forward returns do not materialize in >50% of months where CPI exceeds 3% and funds rate stays under 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over a 24-month rolling window or payroll growth fails to correlate with positive 5-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts dropping below 1 million units signals a severe credit contraction and reduced consumer spending, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 1.0 million units annualized
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in >65% of months where housing starts breach the 1 million unit threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising GDP growth and falling industrial production indicates supply chain bottlenecks and cost-push inflation, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in >55% of observed quarters, invalidating the cost-push compression mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, rising unemployment alongside elevated policy rates signals aggressive demand destruction, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >60% of observations during tightening periods where unemployment rises and funds rate exceeds 3%.
